 {"id":"bd-1ovi8","title":"Task: Implement ensemble loader with user/project override support","description":"Implement user-defined ensemble configuration system with user/project overrides.

## Configuration File Format

### Locations
```
~/.config/ntm/ensembles.yaml
# or project-local
.ntm/ensembles.yaml
```

### Schema
```yaml
ensembles:
  my-security-review:
    display: "Security Review"
    description: "Security-focused code review ensemble"
    modes:
      - id: deductive         # A1
      - id: root-cause        # E2
      - id: worst-case        # G1
      - id: meta-cognitive    # L1
      - id: systems-thinking  # K1
    synthesis:
      strategy: adversarial
      synthesizer_mode: meta-cognitive
    budget:
      per_agent: 5000
      total: 35000
    allow_advanced: true
    agent_distribution:
      claude: 3
      codex: 2
    tags: [security, review]

  extended-architecture:
    extends: architecture-review
    modes:
      - id: formal-verification  # A3
    synthesis:
      strategy: deliberative

aliases:
  sec: my-security-review
  arch: architecture-review
```

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/config.go
type EnsembleConfigFile struct {
    Ensembles map[string]*UserEnsemble `yaml:"ensembles"`
    Aliases   map[string]string        `yaml:"aliases"`
}

type UserEnsemble struct {
    Display           string           `yaml:"display"`
    Description       string           `yaml:"description"`
    Extends           string           `yaml:"extends"`
    Modes             []ModeRef        `yaml:"modes"` // id or code
    Synthesis         SynthesisConfig  `yaml:"synthesis"`
    Budget            BudgetConfig     `yaml:"budget"`
    AllowAdvanced     bool             `yaml:"allow_advanced"`
    AgentDistribution map[string]int   `yaml:"agent_distribution"`
    Tags              []string         `yaml:"tags"`
}

func LoadEnsembleConfig() (*EnsembleConfigFile, error)
func (c *EnsembleConfigFile) GetEnsemble(name string) (*ResolvedEnsemble, error)
func (c *EnsembleConfigFile) ResolveAlias(name string) string
func MergeWithBuiltins(user *EnsembleConfigFile) *EnsembleConfigFile
```

## Validation
- Mode references resolve by id or code
- Strategy values valid (bd-2ztw9)
- Extends references existing ensemble
- No circular extends

## Dependencies
- Built-in ensembles (bd-cph20)
- Mode code support (bd-3806s)
- Strategy definitions (bd-2ztw9)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:41:24.472913894Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T02:57:27.211596389Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T02:57:27.211557906Z","close_reason":"Complete: ensemble_loader.go with multi-source precedence (embedded < user < project), EnsembleRegistry, GlobalEnsembleRegistry singleton, all tests passing","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-2ztw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-35e81","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-cph20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1pt6v","title":"Implement --pane flag for single-pane diagnosis","description":"Implement --pane flag for single-pane diagnosis.

## What

Add --pane=N flag to diagnose a specific pane instead of all panes.

## Use Cases

- Quick check on suspected problem pane
- Debugging a specific agent
- Reduced output when you know where the issue is

## Implementation

```go
func diagnose(session string, paneFilter *int) DiagnoseResult {
    panes := getAllPanes(session)
    
    if paneFilter != nil {
        // Filter to single pane
        panes = filterToPanes(panes, []int{*paneFilter})
        if len(panes) == 0 {
            return errorResult("PANE_NOT_FOUND", paneFilter)
        }
    }
    
    // Continue with diagnosis on filtered panes
    ...
}
```

## Output Difference

With --pane=4:
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "filter": {"pane": 4},
  "panes": {
    "healthy": [],
    "rate_limited": [4]
  },
  "recommendations": [{...single recommendation for pane 4...}]
}
```

## Validation

- Error if pane doesn't exist
- Error if pane is control pane (1) unless --allow-control","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:39:20.631366165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.615891952Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.615850244Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-31e1f diagnose.go - includes --diagnose-pane flag, overall health classification, --diagnose-fix auto-remediation, recommendation generator, and health classification engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pt6v","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2wd98","title":"Unit Tests: Retry failed assignments logic","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for retry failed assignments logic.

## Test Cases

### Single Retry
- Retry specific bead ID
- Bead must be in failed status
- Creates new assignment
- Old assignment marked appropriately

### Retry to Specific Pane
- --to-pane targets specific agent
- Validates pane exists
- Validates pane is idle (or --force)

### Batch Retry
- --retry-failed finds all failed
- --agent filter works
- Respects --limit
- Handles partial success

### Strategy Integration
- Uses strategy for agent selection
- --strategy=quality picks best match
- Falls back when no idle agents

### Error Cases
- Retry non-failed bead -> error
- Retry non-existent bead -> error
- No idle agents -> skip or error

## Logging
- Log retry decisions
- Log old vs new assignment
- Log failures

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-2wih","title":"Task: Add --stagger flag to spawn","description":"## Purpose
Add staggering option to spawn command for rate limit avoidance.

## Flag
ntm spawn myproject --cc=5 --stagger=smart|fixed|none

## Stagger Modes
- smart: Use learned optimal delays from RateLimitTracker
- fixed: Use fixed delay (--stagger-delay=15s)
- none: No staggering (default for backward compatibility)

## Implementation
func (s *SpawnCmd) spawnWithStagger(agents []AgentSpec) error {
    delay := s.rateLimitTracker.GetOptimalDelay(agents[0].Provider)
    
    for i, agent := range agents {
        if i > 0 && s.staggerMode \!= 'none' {
            time.Sleep(delay)
        }
        
        if err := s.spawnAgent(agent); err \!= nil {
            if isRateLimitError(err) {
                s.rateLimitTracker.RecordRateLimit(agent.Provider)
                delay = s.rateLimitTracker.GetOptimalDelay(agent.Provider)
                i-- // Retry
                continue
            }
            return err
        }
        s.rateLimitTracker.RecordSuccess(agent.Provider)
    }
    return nil
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Staggered spawning works
- Retries on rate limit
- Progress shown during spawn
- Default is backward compatible

## Technical Notes
Add flag to existing spawn command","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-5dfye","title":"Task: Redaction spec + pattern catalog","description":"## Goal
Define the canonical set of detection categories + patterns and how they map to user-facing messages and redaction placeholders.

**Explicit stance (v1):** prioritize *secrets*; defer PII (email/phone/SSN/credit-card) behind a future opt-in or stricter safety profile.

## Source of Truth (current implementation)
- Engine: `internal/safety/redaction` (modes: off|warn|redact|block, allowlist support)
- Canonical fixture corpus: `testdata/redaction_fixtures.json` (loaded by `internal/safety/redaction/redaction_test.go`)
- Legacy regex list to migrate away from: `internal/checkpoint/export.go` (`secretPatterns`)

## Canonical Categories (v1)
Use these exact tokens (matches `internal/safety/redaction.Category`):
- `OPENAI_KEY`
- `ANTHROPIC_KEY`
- `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- `GOOGLE_API_KEY`
- `AWS_ACCESS_KEY`
- `AWS_SECRET_KEY`
- `JWT`
- `BEARER_TOKEN`
- `PRIVATE_KEY`
- `DATABASE_URL`
- `PASSWORD`
- `GENERIC_API_KEY`
- `GENERIC_SECRET`

## Pattern Catalog (v1)
The authoritative list is `internal/safety/redaction/patterns.go` (ordered by priority; higher = more specific).

**Provider keys (high priority)**
- `OPENAI_KEY`
  - `sk-...T3BlbkFJ...` (OpenAI base64 marker)
  - `sk-proj-...` (project keys)
- `ANTHROPIC_KEY`
  - `sk-ant-...`
- `GITHUB_TOKEN`
  - `gh[pousr]_...` (classic/oauth/app/user tokens)
  - `github_pat_..._...` (fine-grained PAT)
- `GOOGLE_API_KEY`
  - `AIza...` (Google API key)

**Cloud credentials**
- `AWS_ACCESS_KEY`
  - `AKIA...` (long-lived)
  - `ASIA...` (STS)
- `AWS_SECRET_KEY`
  - assignment forms containing `aws_secret` / `secret_access_key` / `secret_key`

**Auth tokens**
- `JWT`
  - `eyJ... . eyJ... . ...`
- `BEARER_TOKEN`
  - `bearer <token>` (header or inline)

**Other high-signal secrets**
- `PRIVATE_KEY`
  - `-----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY-----`
- `DATABASE_URL`
  - `postgres|mysql|mongodb|redis://user:pass@host...`

**Generic key/value secrets (lower priority)**
- `PASSWORD`
  - `password|passwd|pwd = ...`
- `GENERIC_API_KEY`
  - `*_api_key|apikey = ...`
- `GENERIC_SECRET`
  - `secret|private_key|token = ...`

### Overlap / precedence strategy
- Prefer provider-specific matches over generic matches.
- If overlaps exist, keep the higher-priority match and drop the lower-priority one.
- This behavior is tested in `internal/safety/redaction/redaction_test.go` (overlap + order invariants).

## Redaction Placeholder Strategy
- Placeholder format: `[REDACTED:{CATEGORY}:{hash8}]`
- `hash8` = first 8 lowercase hex chars of `SHA-256("{CATEGORY}:{MATCH}")`
- Hash is deterministic + fixed-length (does not leak the match length).

## UX / Messaging
- `warn`: surface category + where (prompt vs output vs export) + how to override (allowlist / disable category / per-command flag).
- `block`: fail the operation with a clear `error_code` (robot) and guidance.

## False-Positive Strategy + Allowlist Escape Hatch
- Keep generic patterns low priority.
- Allowlist is regex-based and must suppress findings **by match**, not by category.

## Inventory: legacy checkpoint-export patterns → categories
`internal/checkpoint/export.go` currently redacts by replacing matches with `[REDACTED]`. These should be mapped onto the categories above and routed through the shared engine:
- `(?i)(api[_-]?key|apikey) ...` → `GENERIC_API_KEY`
- `(?i)(secret|password|passwd|pwd) ...` → `PASSWORD` (and/or `GENERIC_SECRET` for non-password tokens)
- `(?i)(token|bearer) ...` / `Authorization: Bearer ...` → `BEARER_TOKEN`
- `AKIA...` → `AWS_ACCESS_KEY`
- `aws_secret|aws_access ...` → `AWS_SECRET_KEY` / `AWS_ACCESS_KEY` (split)
- `ghp_...` → `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- `sk-...` → `OPENAI_KEY`
- `sk-ant-...` → `ANTHROPIC_KEY`
- `-----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY-----` → `PRIVATE_KEY`

## Fixtures
- `testdata/redaction_fixtures.json` contains **≥30 true-positive fixtures across categories**, plus negatives + edge cases.
- The file uses the `<<OPENAI_TEST_KEY>>` placeholder to avoid triggering secret scanners; tests substitute a synthetic key.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonFox","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:29.318977840Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:38:25.766543229Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:35:41.927121348Z","close_reason":"Completed: canonical spec + fixture catalog in docs/REDACTION_SPEC.md (includes checkpoint export regex regression set)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5dfye","depends_on_id":"bd-3cgwv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5h1bi","title":"Feature: ntm support-bundle command (zip/tar.gz + manifest)","description":"## Summary
Implement a command to generate a support bundle archive.

## CLI Sketch
- `ntm support-bundle --output bundle.zip`
- `ntm support-bundle --session myproj --panes 1,2 --lines 200 --output bundle.zip`
- `ntm support-bundle --since 2h --output bundle.zip`

## Archive Contents (proposed)
- `manifest.json` (versioned schema + checksums)
- `doctor.json`
- `config_effective.toml`
- `versions.json` (ntm/go/tmux + optional tools)
- `events.jsonl` (bounded window)
- `sessions/<name>/snapshot.json` (optional)
- `sessions/<name>/panes/<pane>.txt` (optional)

## Safety
- Default: redaction enabled (reuse redaction engine).
- Respect privacy mode: if privacy enabled, require explicit override to include content.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Archive is deterministic and validated.

 {"id":"bd-5z3e","title":"E2E Tests: Agent-to-agent handoff workflow","description":"E2E Tests for Agent-to-agent handoff workflow.

## Test Scenarios

1. Basic handoff
   - Spawn agent A, work on task partially
   - Kill agent A, spawn agent B with handoff
   - Verify context received
   - Verify B continues work

2. ntm handoff command
   - Run ntm handoff myproject cc_1 cc_2
   - Verify state captured from cc_1
   - Verify state injected to cc_2
   - Verify cc_2 acknowledges

3. Automatic handoff on crash
   - Enable auto-handoff
   - Simulate agent crash
   - Verify replacement spawned
   - Verify context transferred

4. Multi-hop handoff
   - Agent A -> Agent B -> Agent C
   - Verify context accumulates
   - Verify no info loss

## Detailed Logging
```
[E2E-HANDOFF] Capturing state from SOURCE: N bytes
[E2E-HANDOFF] State includes: N decisions, N files, N blockers
[E2E-HANDOFF] Injecting to TARGET
[E2E-HANDOFF] Target acknowledged handoff
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Basic handoff preserves work context
3. ntm handoff command captures and injects state
4. Auto-handoff triggers on agent crash
5. Multi-hop preserves accumulated context
6. Detailed logging with [E2E-HANDOFF] prefix
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify agent-to-agent handoff
2. Handoff message delivered correctly
3. Context transferred between agents
4. Handoff logged in audit trail
5. Test cleanup removes test sessions
6. Test duration under 5 minutes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:16:47.931743771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:48:02.307025512Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:48:02.306953749Z","close_reason":"Implemented 9 E2E test scenarios covering handoff workflow: basic, multi-hop chain, file changes, resume, output formats, session listing, agent info, blockers, directory structure. ## Widget Design

### Compact View
```
┌─ RCH Build Offload ─────────────┐
│ 🟢 45 builds (30 remote)        │
│ Time saved: ~20 min             │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Expanded View
```
┌─ RCH Status ─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Workers:  builder-1 (🟢)  builder-2 (busy)       │
│                                                   │
│ Session Stats:                                    │
│   Total builds:     45                            │
│   Remote:           30 (67%)                      │
│   Local (fallback): 15 (33%)                      │
│   Time saved:       ~20 minutes                   │
│                                                   │
│ Current: cargo build --release on builder-2      │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Dashboard widget shows build offload status
2. Displays local vs remote build counts
3. Shows time saved via offloading

- CLI is provided by toon_rust (binary: `tru`).
- Prefer `tru --version` for version parsing; fallback to `tru --help` if needed.

- Parses version output
- Used by `--robot-format=auto` to decide availability","notes":"UPDATE per bd-2csfi decision: This task is no longer needed.

 {"id":"bd-6fek","title":"Error Handling Tests: ntm init recovery scenarios","description":"Error Handling Tests for ntm init recovery scenarios.

## Error Scenarios to Test

1. Filesystem errors
   - Permission denied on directory creation
   - Disk full during config write
   - Read-only filesystem
   - Network filesystem latency

2. Config validation errors
   - Invalid TOML syntax recovery
   - Missing required fields
   - Type mismatches in config
   - Circular template references

3. Graceful degradation
   - Continue with defaults if config unreadable
   - Warn but proceed if optional dir creation fails
   - Fallback if flywheel tool unavailable

## Recovery Verification
- Test that partial init can be resumed
- Test that corrupted config is detected and reported
- Test cleanup on failure (no orphaned files)

## Logging Requirements
```
[ERROR] OPERATION: ERROR_MESSAGE
[RECOVER] Attempting: RECOVERY_ACTION
[CLEANUP] Removing: PATH
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 3 error scenario categories tested
2. Filesystem errors handled gracefully
3. Config validation errors reported clearly
4. Graceful degradation falls back to defaults
5. Partial init can be resumed
6. Failed init cleans up orphaned files
7. Detailed logging with [ERROR]/[RECOVER]/[CLEANUP] prefixes","acceptance_criteria":"1. Error handling tests for ntm init
2. Recovery from partial init tested
3. Config file corruption handled
4. Permission errors handled
5. Test cleanup removes test configs
6. Test duration under 3 minutes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldFinch","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:22.883192270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:39:55.589828934Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:39:55.589762741Z","close_reason":"Added init recovery/error-handling tests (readonly target, corrupt config recovery, resume partial init, hooks degrade)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6fek","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6fek","depends_on_id":"bd-ubhl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6mls6","title":"Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble presets' command for preset management","description":"Implement `ntm ensemble presets` command to list available ensembles (built-in + user-defined).

## Command Specification
```
ntm ensemble presets [flags]
  --format string   Output format: table, json, yaml (default: table)
  --verbose         Show full ensemble configurations
```

- `ntm ensemble presets` is an alias for `ntm ensemble list` (shared implementation).
- Primary UX language: **ensemble**; “presets” is a convenience alias.

## Output Requirements
- Include `name`, `display`, `description`, `modes` (id + code), synthesis strategy, budget, tags
- Include `allow_advanced` flag when present
- In verbose mode, expand modes to include names, categories, and tiers

## Dependencies
- Built-in ensembles (bd-cph20)
- Ensemble loader (bd-1ovi8)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lists built-in + user-defined ensembles
- [ ] JSON/YAML outputs are deterministic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:28.550161191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:36:42.595437577Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:36:42.595344604Z","close_reason":"Implemented ntm ensemble presets command with table/json/yaml formats, verbose mode, and tag filtering. Added unit tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6mls6","depends_on_id":"bd-1ovi8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6mls6","depends_on_id":"bd-35e81","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-6mls6","depends_on_id":"bd-cph20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
